patch-notes.title: Site launched
patch-notes.body: The development of this site started. It is a personal site to show my skills and share my professional experience. It is a continuous work in progress.

patch-notes.title: Patch Notes added
patch-notes.body: Added Patch Notes tab to document the progress made on this site.

patch-notes.title: Migrated to React
patch-notes.body: Migrated the site from static HTML pages to a Vite + React SPA. Navigation is now a shared React component, routing handled by React Router.

patch-notes.title: Project data moved to JSON
patch-notes.body: Project cards are now driven by a JSON data file, making it easy to add or update projects without touching the component code.

patch-notes.title: Full Vite + React SPA rebuild
patch-notes.body: Completed a full migration from static HTML/CSS/vanilla JS to a Vite + React single-page application. Work included: converting all pages (Home, About, Resume, Projects, Patch Notes) to React components; building shared Nav and Footer components with mobile hamburger state managed in React; setting up React Router for client-side routing; reorganizing CSS into per-feature files imported by their components; moving project data to a JSON file; and moving all static images to the public/ directory with absolute paths. The old scripts/app.js and all static HTML pages have been retired.

patch-notes.title: UI/UX redesign
patch-notes.body: Complete visual and content overhaul. Introduced a new design system with a consistent dark header, orange accent, and clean typography. Added a sticky glass-effect nav with a full-screen mobile overlay. Rewrote all page copy to be more professional and personal — highlighting QA/tech work, outdoor adventures, travel, and photography. Cleaned up grammar and tone across every page, updated the résumé with accurate dates and polished bullet points, and gave About a proper three-card layout covering the professional, the journey, and the human behind it all.

patch-notes.title: Project filters now functional
patch-notes.body: The filter buttons on the Projects page are now fully wired up. Each project has been tagged by technology, and clicking a filter shows only matching projects. New filters added beyond the original four: #Selenium, #Automation, and #Spring Boot. Filters are derived directly from the project data, so they'll stay in sync automatically as new projects are added. Clicking an active filter deselects it and returns the full list.

patch-notes.title: Gallery page added (WIP)
patch-notes.body: Added a new Gallery section for photos and videos, organized by event. Each event is its own album — click through to browse the full media grid with a lightbox viewer. Content is still being added.

patch-notes.title: Alaska Trip photos added to Gallery
patch-notes.body: Added the first real event to the Gallery — 11 photos from a November 2023 trip to Palmer, Talkeetna, and Anchorage, Alaska.

patch-notes.title: Articles section added
patch-notes.body: Added a new Articles section for writing on automation and engineering topics. Three articles are in the works: Strategy Pattern for Multi Country Support in Automation, Authenticated State in Automation, and Stabilizing Automation Tests with Network Beacons.

patch-notes.title: Game Start demo shop added
patch-notes.body: Added a standalone e-commerce demo page at /demos/gamestart/shop/mybag — a retro-themed mock cart for a fictional game retailer called Game Start. Pre-loaded with three titles, it supports quantity steppers, item removal, and a live-updating order summary. Built as a Playwright automation target with full data-testid coverage.

patch-notes.title: Site polish: hero CTA, stack badges, footer & nav updates
patch-notes.body: Several UX improvements: homepage hero now shows a tech stack badge row (Playwright, Cypress, TypeScript, Python, GitLab CI), a View Résumé CTA, and an updated tagline. Footer email is now a clickable mailto link; Facebook replaced with GitHub and GitLab. Articles nav item hidden until content is ready. Experience year count standardised to 8+ across all pages.

patch-notes.title: no-plan app page added
patch-notes.body: Added a dedicated product page for the no-plan fitness app — the most ambitious project on the site. Six full-bleed sections: vision, features, screenshots, tech stack, and live development roadmap.
